text.content: Learning Analytics on Student Engagement to Enhance Students’ Learning Performance: A Systematic Review

title.plain_text: Learning Analytics on Student Engagement to Enhance Students’ Learning Performance: A Systematic Review

blocks.text: Abstract

text.content: Abstract

rich_text.plain_text: Abstract

blocks.text: Prisma review of 42 papers. There is a limited number of studies performing a multi faceted analysis on online student engagement. It draws from the Redmond et. al 5 types of online engagement.

text.content: Prisma review of 42 papers. There is a limited number of studies performing a multi faceted analysis on online student engagement. It draws from the Redmond et. al 5 types of online engagement.

rich_text.plain_text: Prisma review of 42 papers. There is a limited number of studies performing a multi faceted analysis on online student engagement. It draws from the Redmond et. al 5 types of online engagement.

blocks.text: Introduction

text.content: Introduction

rich_text.plain_text: Introduction

blocks.text: Student engagement is a form of student involvement in activities that are likely to result in higher quality learning and evidence suggests that more engaged students tend to perform better and achieve better conceptual understanding. Online engagement can follow 5 components: social, cognitive, collaborative, behavioural and emotional. Missing aggregate analysis and common issues related with lack of focus and motivation in online learning motivate the study.

text.content: Student engagement is a form of student involvement in activities that are likely to result in higher quality learning and evidence suggests that more engaged students tend to perform better and achieve better conceptual understanding. Online engagement can follow 5 components: social, cognitive, collaborative, behavioural and emotional. Missing aggregate analysis and common issues related with lack of focus and motivation in online learning motivate the study.

rich_text.plain_text: Student engagement is a form of student involvement in activities that are likely to result in higher quality learning and evidence suggests that more engaged students tend to perform better and achieve better conceptual understanding. Online engagement can follow 5 components: social, cognitive, collaborative, behavioural and emotional. Missing aggregate analysis and common issues related with lack of focus and motivation in online learning motivate the study.

blocks.text: It is important to ensure engaging and collaborative environments in online learning environments. Learning analytics allow for collection and analysis of online data and has the potential to improve student learning online.

blocks.text: Student engagement can be measured from the LMS and there has been an established relationship between engagement and performance. Learning content must be designed in a way that promotes 5 types of engagement: social interaction, cognitive skills, behavioral engagement, collaborative learning and emotional engagement.

blocks.text: Social engagement: the forums and other tools that facilitate communication and networking by the students.

blocks.text: Cognitive engagement: understanding complex ideas. It is an active learning process demonstrating participation. Online materials should promote it by encouraging critical thinking.

blocks.text: Collaborative engagement: it is promoted by activities that reflect communication between students. This may come in the form of forums or joining students in groups.

blocks.text: Behaviour engagement: showing interest in learning. This comes in two types: dynamic behaviour and passive behaviour. Active students tend to have positive attitudes towards learning tasks and try to participate in it. This sort of behaviour can be measured using online activities.

blocks.text: Emotional engagement: This domain reflects the attitude towards learning. Attitude, enthusiasm, curiosity, anxiety and pleasure in learning. It has been shown that emotions can promote or distract thinking and decision makig in online environments.

blocks.text: Engagement and Performance on Learning Performance

text.content: Engagement and Performance on Learning Performance

rich_text.plain_text: Engagement and Performance on Learning Performance

blocks.text: Learning analytics has been here for a long time and its use has mainly been focused on higher education. Applications include informing curriculum design, improvements of learning and teaching environments, analysis of engagement and learning performance, thus making LA an interesting option to unobtrusively measure engagement online. Another application is the measurement of student performance - which can also be adapted to develop strategies to influence student performance.

text.content: Learning analytics has been here for a long time and its use has mainly been focused on higher education. Applications include informing curriculum design, improvements of learning and teaching environments, analysis of engagement and learning performance, thus making LA an interesting option to unobtrusively measure engagement online. Another application is the measurement of student performance - which can also be adapted to develop strategies to influence student performance.

blocks.text: The motivation for this study is to perform a systematic literature review of studies about student engagement and student-engagement aspects in online-learning environments.

blocks.text: Methodology

text.content: Methodology

blocks.text: Used PRISMA on empyrical studies, written in English and using LA in Higher Education using keywords such as “student engagement”, “learning analytics” and “online learning” - 159 papers and, at the second filtering stage I would go 42.

blocks.text: Cognitive engagement: feedback and interaction with peers and it has been argued that sharing information in online platforms reflects cognitive engagement. Studies combining multiple perspectives of engagement have tended to lead to more comprehensive measures. With time and more literature, the number of studies including multiple perspectives have been ever increasing.

blocks.text: Most researched type of engagement was behavioral. Some authors have seen the performance of their students increase after receiving interventions flagged by early warning systems.

text.content: Most researched type of engagement was behavioral. Some authors have seen the performance of their students increase after receiving interventions flagged by early warning systems.

blocks.text: Three types of interactions have also been shown to promote cognitive engagement: discussion of questions, quality of student responses and the general learning community. Learning analytics can be used to promote this type of interactions and measure how they are received by the students. However, all of this also reflects caveats from instructional design and learning instructions.

text.content: Three types of interactions have also been shown to promote cognitive engagement: discussion of questions, quality of student responses and the general learning community. Learning analytics can be used to promote this type of interactions and measure how they are received by the students. However, all of this also reflects caveats from instructional design and learning instructions.

Learning analytics can be used to promote this type of interactions and measure how they are received by the students. However, all of this also reflects caveats from instructional design and learning instructions.

blocks.text: Other authors have used the time spent watching a video to assess their learning outcomes. Other authors figured that participation (again, engagement) positively relate with student success and performance.

blocks.text: While the positive relationship between engagement and performance seems to be ever-present, it should be interpreted with caveats. Logs are only a partial picture and there are many other potential factor involved in learning. Moreover, it is possible to achieve better performances while interacting less with the systems.

text.content: While the positive relationship between engagement and performance seems to be ever-present, it should be interpreted with caveats. Logs are only a partial picture and there are many other potential factor involved in learning. Moreover, it is possible to achieve better performances while interacting less with the systems.

rich_text.plain_text: While the positive relationship between engagement and performance seems to be ever-present, it should be interpreted with caveats. Logs are only a partial picture and there are many other potential factor involved in learning. Moreover, it is possible to achieve better performances while interacting less with the systems.

blocks.text: Discussion

text.content: Discussion

rich_text.plain_text: Discussion

blocks.text: 42 total papers, with 26 having distinctive and multifaceted engagement and 15 used LA to enhance learning performance. There are multiple factors that are related with engagement in online activities. People with more active approaches to learning tend to do better. Teacher instructions promote cognitive and behavioral engagement, but not emotional engagement because students tend be distressed by the overlapping of instructions. The authors did not find any studies that evaluate engagement across the five dimensions and argue for more multi-faceted works.

text.content: 42 total papers, with 26 having distinctive and multifaceted engagement and 15 used LA to enhance learning performance. There are multiple factors that are related with engagement in online activities. People with more active approaches to learning tend to do better. Teacher instructions promote cognitive and behavioral engagement, but not emotional engagement because students tend be distressed by the overlapping of instructions. The authors did not find any studies that evaluate engagement across the five dimensions and argue for more multi-faceted works.

rich_text.plain_text: 42 total papers, with 26 having distinctive and multifaceted engagement and 15 used LA to enhance learning performance. There are multiple factors that are related with engagement in online activities. People with more active approaches to learning tend to do better. Teacher instructions promote cognitive and behavioral engagement, but not emotional engagement because students tend be distressed by the overlapping of instructions. The authors did not find any studies that evaluate engagement across the five dimensions and argue for more multi-faceted works.

blocks.text: An interesting application can be intelligent tutoring and adaptations - in other words personalized learning.

blocks.text: Conclusion

text.content: Conclusion

rich_text.plain_text: Conclusion

blocks.text: No studies on the 5 types of engagement identified by the authors. Main applications of LA are predicting of performance to enhance student outcomes. Important gap in terms of student engagement in the context of online learning and a more multi-faceted approach can lead to mor comprehensive solutions to the most common issues with online learning.
